comment_text.sn4.5:1.1: This event is also told in the Vinaya (Kd 1:11.1.2). It is regarded as an archetypal event for all Buddhas, and hence is told in the context of the past Buddha Vipassī (DN 14:3.22.4).
comment_text.sn4.5:2.8: Senā was where the Buddha attained Awakening, the pilgrimage site of Bodhgaya (MN 26:17.1). The events on his return to Uruvelā are told in the Vinaya (Kd 1:15.1.1). The Buddha encountered three Kassapa brothers who were fire-worshiping matted-hair ascetics, and converted them with their retinues.

root_text.sn4.5:1.1: Ekaṁ samayaṁ bhagavā bārāṇasiyaṁ viharati isipatane migadāye.
root_text.sn4.5:2.1: “Muttāhaṁ, bhikkhave, sabbapāsehi ye dibbā ye ca mānusā.
root_text.sn4.5:2.2: Tumhepi, bhikkhave, muttā sabbapāsehi ye dibbā ye ca mānusā.
root_text.sn4.5:2.3: Caratha, bhikkhave, cārikaṁ bahujanahitāya bahujanasukhāya lokānukampāya atthāya hitāya sukhāya devamanussānaṁ.
root_text.sn4.5:2.5: Desetha, bhikkhave, dhammaṁ ādikalyāṇaṁ majjhekalyāṇaṁ pariyosānakalyāṇaṁ sātthaṁ sabyañjanaṁ kevalaparipuṇṇaṁ parisuddhaṁ brahmacariyaṁ pakāsetha.
root_text.sn4.5:2.6: Santi sattā apparajakkhajātikā, assavanatā dhammassa parihāyanti.
root_text.sn4.5:2.8: Ahampi, bhikkhave, yena uruvelā senānigamo tenupasaṅkamissāmi dhammadesanāyā”ti.
root_text.sn4.5:2.9: Atha kho māro pāpimā yena bhagavā tenupasaṅkami; upasaṅkamitvā bhagavantaṁ gāthāya ajjhabhāsi:

translation_text.sn4.5:1.1: At one time the Buddha was staying near Varanasi, in the deer park at Isipatana.
translation_text.sn4.5:1.2: There the Buddha addressed the mendicants,
translation_text.sn4.5:2.1: “Mendicants, I am freed from all snares, both human and heavenly.
translation_text.sn4.5:2.2: You are also freed from all snares, both human and heavenly.
translation_text.sn4.5:2.3: Wander forth, mendicants, for the welfare and happiness of the people, out of sympathy for the world, for the benefit, welfare, and happiness of gods and humans.
translation_text.sn4.5:2.4: Let not two go by one road.
translation_text.sn4.5:2.5: Teach the Dhamma that’s good in the beginning, good in the middle, and good in the end, meaningful and well-phrased. And reveal a spiritual practice that’s entirely full and pure.
translation_text.sn4.5:2.6: There are beings with little dust in their eyes. They’re in decline because they haven’t heard the teaching.
translation_text.sn4.5:2.7: There will be those who understand the teaching!
translation_text.sn4.5:2.8: I will travel to the town of Senā in Uruvelā in order to teach the Dhamma.”
translation_text.sn4.5:2.9: Then Māra the Wicked went up to the Buddha and addressed him in verse:
translation_text.sn4.5:3.3: You’re bound by the great bond:
translation_text.sn4.5:4.3: I’m freed from the great bonds;
